text.title: Guardrails Implementation Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for a production guardrails pipeline class.

rule.statement: Thresholds + toggles live in a GuardrailConfig dataclass loaded at startup. Never hardcode thresholds inside validator functions.
rule.rationale: Centralised config makes the security posture auditable and tunable without redeploy.

rule.statement: Input pipeline orders checks cheap-first: rule-based regex (µs) → API moderation (~100ms) → optional LLM check (last).
rule.rationale: Layering by cost stops the slow checks from running on inputs that fast checks already reject.

rule.statement: Output pipeline separates validators (boolean accept/block) from filters (transform). Validators run first; filters only apply if output is not blocked.
rule.rationale: Mixing validation and filtering makes audit impossible: "was it blocked or modified?" must be answerable.

rule.statement: Guardrail check exceptions fail loudly — never `except Exception: pass`. A failed check returns a violation; a crashing check raises.
rule.rationale: Silent swallow converts safety failures into invisible pass-throughs.

rule.statement: For latency-sensitive deployments, parallel-execute independent input checks via ThreadPoolExecutor / asyncio.gather. Collect all violations before deciding block/pass.
rule.rationale: Sequential adds latency = sum(checks); parallel = max(check).

rule.statement: Every call emits a structured violation_log: {trace_id, layer, violation_type, severity, decision, latency_ms} to telemetry.
rule.rationale: Per-layer rates expose silent rot and let SRE tune without guessing.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire the GuardrailsPipeline per procedure.
Internal-only or prototype or sub-500ms budget → skip.
